title: IPTG-induced Overexpression in E. coli
steps: Protein Expression (optimised for inclusion bodies) Take volume (100 ml) LB+ampicillin Add 1 ml of the overnight culture Let the culture grow to an OD 600 of 0.6 at 37°C/180 rpm Induce with 0.5 mM IPTG (1M Stock) --> 50 µl Incubate for 2 hours at 37°C/ 180 rpm Centrifuge the culture for 10 min at 4000xg / 4°C Store cell pellet without supernatant at -80°C

Purification of inclusion bodies Thaw cell pellet on ice Resuspend pellet in 1 ml water (2x50 ml pellets from 100 ml culture can be resuspended together) Transfer to 2 ml reaction tubes Centrifuge at maximum speed of a desktop centrifuge at 4°C for 15 minutes Discard supernatant Resuspend pellet in 0.5 ml BPER by vortexting Repeat vortexing every 2 minutes for 15 minutes Centrifuge at maximum speed of a desktop centrifuge at 4°C for 15 minutes Store the supernatant (BPER) in seperate reaction tubes (on ice) Dilute 1 ml BPER with 9 ml water to create 1/10 BPER Wash the pellet with 1 ml 1/10 BPER (can be done by vortexing for 3x1 minute) Centrifuge at maximum speed of a desktop centrifuge at 4°C for 15 minutes Store the supernatant (1/10 BPER) in seperate reaction tubes (on ice) Resuspend pellet in 200 μl 50 mM HEPES pH 7.4, 150 mM KCl, 10% Glycerol (If you do not intend to use your protein for activity assays, water can also be used) Put samples on SDS gel or store at -20°C. On an SDS gel mix 0.5-1 µl of sample + 4 µl 5x SDS buffer and put on the gel.
creator: Igem Dusseldorf
